obligations.reason: The scope exposes top-level debug_req_i and csr_regfile debug state/output signals. Check whether the debug requester can force privileged debug mode or PC redirection without an authorization guard, and distinguish this from mere presence of debug logic by proving reachability to debug state/control outputs.
obligations.subject: external debug interface/debug_req_i
obligations.operation: enter debug mode / redirect PC to debug handler
obligations.object: debug_mode_q, debug_mode_o, set_debug_pc_o, debug PC control state
obligations.expected_guard: debug entry should be mediated by debug authorization/lifecycle policy and should not be asserted solely by an external request; entry should also respect pipeline/AMO safety conditions
state_conditions_to_check: clear condition via dret or reset
interaction with amo_valid_commit gating in ariane
whether debug_mode_q suppresses or permits normal CSR/commit behavior
required_evidence_slots: operation: debug entry or set_debug_pc assertion
expected guard: debug authorization/lifecycle plus AMO/pipeline gating
observed RTL condition for setting debug state
path/reachability across ariane and csr_regfile ports
knowledge_used: state lifecycle: set condition, use site, mode transition
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer is truncated around assignments, so exact set/clear logic must be inspected in source.

obligations.reason: Debug CSR addresses are defined in riscv_pkg and backing registers exist in csr_regfile. These are protected debug assets and must not be exposed through the normal CSR path without debug-mode mediation.
obligations.subject: software-visible CSR instruction interface
obligations.operation: read/write debug CSRs through normal CSR instruction interface
obligations.object: CSR_DCSR, CSR_DPC, CSR_DSCRATCH0, CSR_DSCRATCH1; dpc_q, dscratch0_q, dscratch1_q
obligations.expected_guard: debug CSRs should be readable/writable only in debug mode or by an authorized debug context; normal U/S/M CSR instructions should not freely access dcsr/dpc/dscratch unless architecture explicitly allows the access and traps otherwise
obligations.candidate_path: CSR decode/write path csr_addr_i/csr_wdata_i/fu_op -> csr_we/csr_wdata -> dcsr/dpc/dscratch registers
state_conditions_to_check: read_access_exception asserted or not for non-debug access
required_evidence_slots: subject: normal CSR instruction path/requesting privilege mode
operation: read and write decode for 0x7b0-0x7b3
expected guard: debug_mode_q or equivalent authorization and privilege trap
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect CSR read/write case statements at lines around 157, 334, 620, 745, 926.

obligations.reason: Machine CSRs configure privilege, trap routing, interrupt enable, and memory-access policy. The visible CSR regfile has a generic CSR address/write data path; check that privilege-mode checks gate all machine CSR read/write updates and not only read_access_exception.
obligations.subject: software CSR access path from decoded instructions
obligations.object: CSR_MSTATUS, CSR_MEDELEG, CSR_MIDELEG, CSR_MIE, CSR_MTVEC, CSR_MSCRATCH, CSR_MEPC, CSR_MCAUSE, CSR_MTVAL, CSR_MIP and mstatus fields mprv/tvm/tw/tsr/sum/mxr
obligations.expected_guard: machine-level CSRs and privilege-control fields should be writable only by machine privilege or equivalent trap/exception mediation; U/S writes must be rejected, and read restrictions should match CSR privilege encoding
obligations.candidate_path: csr_addr_i/csr_wdata_i/fu_op -> csr_we -> mstatus fields and m-mode CSRs
state_conditions_to_check: reset default of mstatus and related privilege fields
required_evidence_slots: subject: CSR requester privilege mode/current privilege state
object: machine CSR register or mstatus field
expected guard: CSR address privilege decode and trap exception
observed write-enable gating per CSR case
path from id/commit CSR controls to csr_regfile
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer does not explicitly list current privilege signal in csr_regfile ports; inspect type ports and internal privilege state.

obligations.reason: SATP and translation-enable outputs directly affect memory isolation. The design exposes tvm_o and translation outputs, so verify SATP/SFENCE-related permission checks and whether TVM blocks lower-privilege changes.
obligations.subject: software CSR access path / lower privilege modes
obligations.object: CSR_SATP, satp mode/asid/ppn state, en_translation_o, en_ld_st_translation_o, satp_ppn_o, asid_o
obligations.expected_guard: SATP writes and translation-control changes should be allowed only from S/M privilege as specified, and should be blocked/trapped when machine TVM policy forbids S-mode SATP/SFENCE changes
state_conditions_to_check: mode transition via mret/sret affecting translation outputs
required_evidence_slots: operation: write SATP or execute SFENCE/VMA-related control
object: translation enable, ASID, PPN state
expected guard: privilege check plus TVM mediation
obligations.uncertainty: SATP backing signal name may be embedded in structured CSR state not listed; inspect csr_regfile cases.

obligations.reason: Status bits can relax access checks or constrain lower modes. Verify all write paths, including SSTATUS aliases, are privilege-gated and mask protected fields so lower modes cannot modify machine-only policy.
obligations.subject: software CSR access path
obligations.operation: change memory/privilege access policy through status CSRs
obligations.object: mstatus/sstatus permission bits: SUM, MXR, MPRV, TVM, TW, TSR and related outputs
obligations.expected_guard: writes to fields that relax memory or supervisor controls should be restricted to sufficiently privileged modes and should preserve architectural masking of lower-privilege writes
obligations.candidate_path: csr_addr_i/csr_wdata_i/fu_op -> csr_we -> sstatus/mstatus SUM/MXR/MPRV/TVM/TW/TSR fields -> outputs sum_o/mxr_o/tvm_o/tw_o/tsr_o/en_ld_st_translation_o
state_conditions_to_check: mret/sret updates to privilege stack fields
dirty_fp_state updates interacting with status fields
required_evidence_slots: object: specific status policy fields and outputs
expected guard: M/S privilege plus field-level write mask
observed write mask and alias behavior
obligations.uncertainty: Need field-level update masks from csr_regfile write cases.

obligations.reason: Counters and performance events can leak execution activity and are commonly mediated by counter-enable CSRs. Visible ports include perf_data_i/o and perf_we_o; check read/write accessibility by privilege and counter-enable state.
obligations.subject: software CSR access path
obligations.expected_guard: user/supervisor reads of counters and performance events should be controlled by counter-enable CSRs (mcounteren/scounteren or equivalent) and writes/configuration should be privileged
obligations.candidate_path: CSR_CYCLE/CSR_TIME/CSR_INSTRET/perf CSR address decode -> csr_rdata_o/perf_data_i and perf_we_o
required_evidence_slots: operation: counter read or performance configuration write
expected guard: counter-enable and privilege checks
observed read mux and write-enable behavior
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect whether mcounteren/scounteren signals exist or are omitted in implementation.

obligations.reason: Cache controls are implementation-specific privileged configuration. The scope defines custom CSR_ICACHE/CSR_DCACHE and outputs to cache/controller logic; verify write mediation and no lower-privilege bypass.
obligations.subject: software CSR access path
obligations.object: CSR_ICACHE, CSR_DCACHE, icache_q, dcache_q, icache_en_o, dcache_en_o, icache_flush_ctrl_cache, dcache_flush_ctrl_cache
obligations.expected_guard: cache enable/disable and cache flush controls that affect memory-system behavior should be writable only by privileged software and not by U-mode or unauthorized CSR aliases
obligations.candidate_path: csr_addr_i/csr_wdata_i/fu_op -> csr_we -> CSR_DCACHE/CSR_ICACHE registers -> icache_en_o/dcache_en_o/flush controls
required_evidence_slots: operation: write custom cache control CSR or trigger flush
expected guard: privileged CSR access check
observed output use in ariane/controller/cache path
obligations.uncertainty: Custom CSR privilege rules must be inferred from RTL/address decode and implementation intent.

obligations.reason: mret/sret/dret are privilege-changing operations. Check that lower modes cannot trigger protected returns, that dret is only honored in debug mode, and that the output PC source cannot be selected from protected EPC/debug PC without the proper state.
obligations.subject: software instruction path / decoded system instructions
obligations.operation: return from exception/debug and change PC/privilege state
obligations.object: mepc_q, sepc_q, dpc_q, epc_o, eret_o, debug_mode_q
obligations.expected_guard: returns from exception/debug mode should be permitted only in the corresponding privilege/debug state and should restore PC/privilege from the correct protected EPC register
state_conditions_to_check: tsr_o affecting sret in lower mode
reset and update of epc registers
required_evidence_slots: subject: decoded return instruction requester/current mode
object: protected EPC/DPC and privilege state
expected guard: current mode/debug_mode validation plus TVM/TSR where applicable
PC selection path to ariane frontend/controller
obligations.uncertainty: Return decode likely in ID stage and CSR regfile; need inspect both modules/signals.
